title: artemisia chamaemelifolia subsp. chamaemelifolia
intro: Artemisia chamaemelifolia subsp. chamaemelifolia, commonly known as Southern Wormwood or Small Mugwort, is a subspecies of perennial herb in the daisy family that grows in the southeastern regions of North America. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Artemisia chamaemelifolia subsp. chamaemelifolia include its use as a digestive aid, a treatment for fever and sore throats, and as a natural remedy for skin conditions and wounds. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as an infusion or tincture, and can also be used in herbal teas, salves, and poultices to harness its medicinal properties.
